description: Negative + positive conformance vectors for verifyProvenanceOffline() (lib/provenance/chain.js). The cryptographic bundles themselves are minted live in tests/provenance-chain.test.ts because the proofs must be real Ed25519 signatures over canonical bytes; this file is the AUTHORITATIVE catalogue of the attacks every conformant verifier MUST reject (valid:false) and the one well-formed bundle it MUST accept (valid:true). Each id here is asserted by name in the test.

must_reject.title: Broken inter-hop link
must_reject.attack: delegation_chain[i].parent_ref / delegator does not equal delegation_chain[i-1].delegatee. Authority is spliced across two unrelated delegations.

must_reject.title: Tampered delegation (scope/cap/expiry not the signed bytes)
must_reject.attack: The delegation proof signature is valid over some bytes, but the link's scope/max_value_usd/expires_at JSON fields were widened AFTER signing, so the visible fields are not the canonical bytes the proof covers.

must_reject.title: Key-substituted delegation proof
must_reject.attack: The proof verifies under an attacker key (att.public_key) that is NOT the key bound to the named delegator. Signature math passes; key binding fails.

must_reject.title: Out-of-root-scope action (root scope must be DERIVED)
must_reject.attack: Executed action_type is outside what the ROOT receipt actually authorized. A verifier that hardcodes root scope to '*' would accept it.

must_reject.title: Empty delegation_chain, action outside root authority
must_reject.attack: delegation_chain is empty (direct human-to-action), but the executed action_type is not within the root receipt's authority. A verifier that only constrains the action when a chain exists would accept it.

must_reject.title: Irreversible action mislabeled execution.irreversible:false
must_reject.attack: An irreversible action sets execution.irreversible=false to drop the per-action approval requirement. Reversibility is not independently asserted, so the verifier must default to requiring approval.

must_reject.title: Per-document human-class override widening 'human'
must_reject.attack: root_signoff.human_key_classes is set by the producer to include the non-human software class (e.g. 'B') so a non-human signoff counts as the root human. The verifier must use its own humanKeyClasses only.

must_reject.title: Expired delegation link
must_reject.attack: A delegation link's expires_at is in the past relative to the verification reference time.

must_reject.title: Value-cap escalation via null cap on a middle hop (depth>=2)
must_reject.attack: A middle delegation sets max_value_usd:null, which must INHERIT the parent's effective cap, never re-open an unbounded cap. A deeper hop then grants a value far above the original cap. A verifier that carries the raw null forward instead of the effective inherited cap accepts the escalation (DelegateCannotExceedPrincipal / spec 4.2).

must_accept.title: Well-formed provenance chain
must_accept.shape: Root human (Class A) signoff -> one scope-narrowing delegation whose proof signs its own canonical fields under the delegator key -> per-action human (Class A) approval -> execution hash-bound to the approval. Executed action is within both the leaf and the derived root scope.
